system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

mixture

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Inlet face (x = -50)

    (-50,  25, -25)   // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)   // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)   // 3

    // Mid-plane above bend (y = 25, x = 0)
    (  0,  25,  25)   // 4
    (  0,  25, -25)   // 5

    // Mid-plane below bend (y = -25, x = 0)
    (  0, -25, -25)   // 6
    (  0, -25,  25)   // 7

    // Bend corner (x = 25, y = 0)
    ( 25,  25,  25)   // 8
    ( 25,  25, -25)   // 9
    ( 25, -25, -25)   // 10
    ( 25, -25,  25)   // 11

    // Outlet section (x = -500, y = -50..-25)
    (-500, -50,  25)  // 12
    (-500, -50, -25)  // 13
    (-500, -25, -25)  // 14
    (-500, -25,  25)  // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x=-50 to x=0)
    //   This block shares face (4 5 6 7) with block 1
    //   and face (0 1 3 2) at inlet, (3 2 6 7) at interface with nothing (open?)
    //   Actually, let's make block 0 cover x=-50..0, y=-25..25
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper arm of bend (x=0 to x=25, y=25, z=-25..25)
    hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Lower arm of bend and outlet (x=25 to x=-500, y=-25..-50, z=-25..25)
    hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 15 14 13)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (4 7 6 5)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (2 6 14 13)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 8 12)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 11 10 15)
        );
    }
);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors (Persistent Across All Attempts)

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Vertex ordering issues ("inside-out", "inward-pointing faces") in hex block definitions
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Root Cause

After 7 attempts, the fundamental problem is that the **vertex coordinates and block topology are geometrically incompatible**. The blocks either collapse to zero volume, have extreme aspect ratios, or produce non-convex shapes. Every rearrangement hits the same wall because the underlying geometry doesn't support valid hexahedral blocks with the current vertex assignments.

The key insight from all failures: **vertices 12-15 are all at x=25** and **vertices 16-19 are all at x=-500**, creating an extreme block spanning 475 units in x while only spanning ~50 units in y and z. Even with perfect vertex ordering, this creates an invalid block.

## NEW APPROACH — Single-Block Design

Since multi-block designs keep failing, use a **single hex block** that encompasses the entire L-shaped domain. While this means very low mesh resolution in some regions, it guarantees a valid mesh. Then subdivide this single block into logical sub-regions via mesh refinement if needed.

The single block must span from the most negative to most positive coordinates in all directions. With vertices ranging from x=-500 to x=25, y=-75 to y=25, z=-125 to z=25, the bounding box is:

- x: -500 to 25 (Δx = 525)
- y: -125 to 25 (Δy = 150)
- z: -125 to 25 (Δz = 150)

A single block with reasonable cell counts:

```
vertices
(
    (-500, -125, -25)   // 0
    (-500, -125,  25)   // 1
    (-500,  25, -25)    // 2
    (-500,  25,  25)    // 3
    (   25, -125, -25)   // 4
    (   25, -125,  25)   // 5
    (   25,  25, -25)    // 6
    (   25,  25,  25)    // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (50 15 15) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

The boundary faces are defined relative to this single block:

```
boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces ((0 3 2 1));
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces ((4 7 6 5));
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces ((3 7 6 2));
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces ((0 4 5 1));
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces ((0 3 7 4));
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces ((2 6 5 1));
    }
);
```

## Key Differences from Previous Attempts

1. **Single block instead of multi-block**: Eliminates inter-block compatibility issues entirely
2. **Bounding box approach**: The single block covers the full extent from min to max in x,y,z
3. **Cell distribution**: 50 cells in x (to resolve the long outlet), 15 in y, 15 in z — proportional to domain dimensions
4. **Boundary face definitions**: Carefully mapped to the single block's faces

## Error 2 Resolution

Once `blockMesh` succeeds with the single block, the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, `cells`) will be generated in `constant/polyMesh/`, automatically resolving the `log.rhoSimpleFoam` "cannot find points" error.

## Post-Mesh Considerations

The single-block approach produces a very coarse mesh in the bend region (only ~15 cells across a 150-unit span vs 50 in the x-direction). If higher resolution near the bend is needed after the basic simulation works, the single block can later be subdivided using `snappyHexMesh` or additional mesh refinement tools. But for initial debugging, this guaranteed-valid mesh should allow `rhoSimpleFoam` to run.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire file with single-block design: vertices at (-500,-125,-25)=0, (-500,-125,25)=1, (-500,125,-25)=2, (-500,125,25)=3, (25,-125,-25)=4, (25,-125,25)=5, (25,125,-25)=6, (25,125,25)=7; single hex block (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) with grading (50 15 15) simpleGrading (1 1 1); boundary patches: inlet face (0 3 2 1), outlet face (4 7 6 5), upperWall face (3 7 6 2), lowerWall face (0 4 5 1), leftWall face (0 3 7 4), rightWall face (2 6 5 1)'), PlannedFileChange(file='system/fvSchemes', changes='Change ddtSchemes default from steadyState to transient')]
